description:
You are acting as a security engineer who is rotating application-wide TLS credentials that live under the hidden directory 
/home/user/.secrets.  
The directory already contains the following three filesystem objects:

1. /home/user/.secrets/old_key.pem      – the previous private key file  
2. /home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem      – the freshly-generated private key file  
3. /home/user/.secrets/active_key.pem   – a symbolic link that **currently** points to old_key.pem

Goal  
Replace the target of the symbolic link /home/user/.secrets/active_key.pem so that, after the change, it resolves to  
/home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem.

Constraints  
• Accomplish the retargeting in a single terminal command (no intermediate CD, RM, or MV steps).  
• Do NOT delete and recreate the link; atomically overwrite it.  
• You do not have root privileges; keep everything inside /home/user.  

Verification log (mandatory)  
After you have updated the link, capture proof in a text file called /home/user/rotation.log.  
The log file must contain exactly one line, **no extra spaces or newline characters before or after**.  
That line must be the absolute, canonical path that the symbolic link finally resolves to.  
Example format (do not include the square brackets):

/home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem

Nothing else should appear in rotation.log—no timestamps, no labels, no additional lines.

Success criteria the grader will check  
1. /home/user/.secrets/active_key.pem exists and is a symbolic link.  
2. readlink -f /home/user/.secrets/active_key.pem returns /home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem.  
3. /home/user/rotation.log exists, is a regular text file, and its sole line is exactly  
   /home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem  

Carry out the rotation now.
truth:
Initial state (pre-task)  
Directory: /home/user/.secrets  
Files and link:  
• old_key.pem – plain-text file containing the string "OLD_KEY_PLACEHOLDER\n"  
• new_key.pem – plain-text file containing the string "NEW_KEY_PLACEHOLDER\n"  
• active_key.pem – symbolic link pointing to /home/user/.secrets/old_key.pem

Expected final state after correct agent actions  
1. The symbolic link active_key.pem exists and points to /home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem  
   (link content must be either absolute or relative, but readlink -f must resolve to the absolute path above).  
2. File /home/user/rotation.log exists with permissions 0644 and content:  
   Line 1: /home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem  
   (There must be exactly one terminating newline character.)

Automated test procedure  
• [  -L /home/user/.secrets/active_key.pem ] &&  
  [ "$(readlink -f /home/user/.secrets/active_key.pem)" = "/home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem" ]  
• [ "$(wc -l < /home/user/rotation.log)" -eq 1 ] &&  
  [ "$(cat /home/user/rotation.log)" = "/home/user/.secrets/new_key.pem" ]
name: task_000000_a4dbbb9b
